records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Gerrymandered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Gerrymandering.]

defs: To divide (a State) into districts for the choice of representatives, in an unnatural and unfair way, with a view to give a political party an advantage over its opponent.
notes: ☞ This was done in Massachusetts at a time when Elbridge Gerry was governor, and was attributed to his influence, hence the name; though it is now known that he was opposed to the measure.
